text: The surgeon retracts the bladder upwards towards the twelve o'clock position while manipulating the vagina using a prograsper. Two needle drivers are visible in the surgical field, indicating preparation for suturing or tissue manipulation.
The surgeon uses a V-lock suture and a 3-0 absorbable suture to start the placation distally, addressing the pulvocervical fascia defect.

The surgeon uses a prograsper to release tension during the placation process, moving from left to right and distal to proximal. Depending on the patient's anatomy, seven to twelve placations are performed.
The surgeon threads a suture through the distal aspect of the right utero sacral ligament, joining it to the vaginal tissue.
The surgeon places the surgical mesh on the anterior and posterior aspects of the hernia repair site, ensuring proper coverage and fixation.
